correct_explanation: irregular global memory accesses reduce load efficiency and make memory traffic dominate runtime
raw_response:
MEMORY_BOUND: NO
REASONING: The DRAM bandwidth utilization is only 0.01%, indicating the kernel is barely touching off-chip memory, likely because the working set fits in cache and loads are efficiently served from L1/L2. Meanwhile, each thread performs a large number of arithmetic operations (8 unrolled iterations with ~7 FLOPs each per step) relative to only two global loads per step, giving a very high compute-to-memory ratio. This makes the kernel compute-bound, dominated by the long chain of dependent floating-point operations in the accumulator rather than by memory access.
